title: polygonum lapathifolium subsp. linicola
intro: Polygonum lapathifolium subsp. linicola, also known as a subspecies of the Polygonum lapathifolium plant, is a perennial herb belonging to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Polygonum lapathifolium subsp. linicola include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with some traditional applications involving topical application of its leaves to treat various skin ailments.
